title: Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2025
short_title: Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2025
stage_label: Reported (Amended) by the Committee on Judiciary. H. Rept. 119-337.
plain_summary: This bill would let people with permits to carry hidden guns in their home state also carry those guns in other states that allow hidden guns. The person must follow the rules of the state they are visiting. If someone stops them from carrying their gun when they should be allowed to, they can take that person to court. The bill does not let people carry guns in places where the owners say no guns are allowed.
last_action_text: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 289.
subjects: Wilderness and natural areas, wildlife refuges, wild rivers, habitats

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Reciprocity for the Carrying of Certain Concealed Firearms
sections.text: This section changes federal law to create new rules about carrying concealed handguns across state lines. If you have a valid permit or license to carry a concealed gun from your home state, and you have a photo identification card, and federal law does not ban you from having guns, then you can carry your concealed handgun in other states too. This works in any state that either allows its own residents to get concealed carry permits or does not ban concealed carry by its residents for lawful purposes. You cannot carry machine guns or destructive devices under this rule. However, states can still let private property owners ban guns on their property. States can also ban guns in government buildings and on government property. If you follow these rules, police cannot arrest you just for carrying a concealed gun unless they have probable cause to believe you are breaking the law. If you show valid documents as required by this law, that is strong evidence that you are following the rules. If you are charged with a crime and win your case by using this law as your defense, the court must make the government pay your lawyer fees. You can also sue anyone, including state or local governments, who violates your rights under this law. If you win such a lawsuit, the court will order them to pay damages and your lawyer fees. An identification document means any official document made by the United States government, a state, or local government that is used to identify people. A handgun includes any magazine for the gun and any bullets loaded in the gun or magazine. People carrying guns under this law are not subject to the federal Gun Free School Zones Act. They can also carry their guns in national parks, wildlife refuges, and other federal lands that are open to the public. This law adds a new section to federal criminal law and updates the table of contents. If any part of this law is found unconstitutional by a court, the rest of the law will still be in effect. These changes will start ninety days after this law is signed.
